intro: realestate.com.au Scraper turns any search on Australia's largest property portal — buy, rent, or sold, including map-drawn searches — into structured data: price, address, beds/baths/parking, land size, agent contacts, and photos. Paste a search URL for a full result set, or a single property URL for one fully detailed record.
useCases: Pull every listing matching a price, size, or location filter to compare deals across suburbs for investment research.
Track newly listed or price-changed properties in a competitor's patch as an agency.
Build a dataset of sold prices and current asking prices for a region to analyze market trends over time.
Collect agent and agency contact details attached to active listings for lead generation.
Monitor rental listings and pricing in a specific area or drawn map region.

faq.q: Do I need a realestate.com.au account to use this?
faq.a: No. Both search URLs and individual property URLs work without logging in.

faq.q: Can I scrape buy, rent, and sold listings, not just one type?
faq.a: Yes — buy, rent, and sold are all supported, including sold-specific sorting like sale date or sale price.

faq.q: What's the difference between a fast run and a fully detailed one?
faq.a: With fetchPropertyDetails off, you get the fast, cheap data already returned by the search. Turning it on fetches the full detail page for every listing — complete feature list, inspection/auction times, and full agency contacts — at a higher per-listing rate. Individual property URLs always return full detail regardless of this setting.

Pricing is per listing, tiered by plan: from $0.0005 per plain listing (Gold plan) plus an optional detail add-on. 1,000 listings without details costs about $0.50 on the Gold plan; with full details, about $1.30.

faq.q: What happens if a run gets interrupted?
faq.a: The actor checkpoints its progress as it works through each search URL, so a crash or manual abort resumes from the last completed page on the next attempt — you don't lose progress or get re-charged for listings already delivered.

faq.q: Does it support map-drawn area searches?
faq.a: Yes — paste a URL from a hand-drawn or pinned map search on the site and it's handled the same as any other search URL.
